scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Weed killer - enters through leaves and kills the whole plant
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays in soil for many months, sometimes over a year
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Stops the plant from making proteins it needs to survive
scientific_profile.Global Status: Probably causes cancer (WHO) - Banned in 20+ countries

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Extremely dangerous insect poison - kills on contact and through eating
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Paralyzes the nervous system of insects and humans alike
scientific_profile.Global Status: Most dangerous category (WHO) - Banned in 60+ countries

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Nerve-attacking insect killer - absorbed by the plant
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Blocks nerve signals - insects cannot move or breathe
scientific_profile.Global Status: Possibly causes cancer (EPA) - Restricted in Europe

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Chemical fertilizer - gives quick burst of Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Dissolves quickly in days - washes into groundwater
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Forces fast growth but poisons water sources with nitrates
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - but causes major water pollution

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Fungus killer - enters the plant and spreads inside
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Stops fungus from building its cell walls
scientific_profile.Global Status: Messes with hormones - Approval cancelled in Europe (2019)

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Potassium salt fertilizer - gives crops potassium
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays a few weeks in soil
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Too much makes soil salty - crops struggle to drink water
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - no major bans

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Strong insect killer - made from soil bacteria compounds
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Breaks down fast, 1-7 days in sunlight
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Paralyzes insect muscles - they stop eating and die
scientific_profile.Global Status: Safer for humans - but extremely toxic to water creatures

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Powerful insect and termite killer
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays 4-12 months in soil - very persistent
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Blocks nerve signals - insects get paralyzed and die
scientific_profile.Global Status: Possibly causes cancer (EPA) - Banned in Europe and Vietnam

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Nerve-attacking insect killer - enters through plant roots and leaves
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Blocks nerve signals - breathing stops in insects
scientific_profile.Global Status: Approval cancelled in Europe (2019)

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Fungus killer - sprayed on leaves to prevent disease
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Breaks down into ETU - a chemical that damages thyroid and DNA
scientific_profile.Global Status: Probable cancer cause (EPA B2) - Cancelled in Europe (2021)

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Fungus killer - soaks into the plant and spreads
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Stops cells from dividing - damages reproductive cells in humans too
scientific_profile.Global Status: Changes DNA - Banned in Europe for causing infertility

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Natural insect killer - made from soil bacteria
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Overstimulates insect nerves - they stop eating
scientific_profile.Global Status: Approved for organic farming - safest synthetic option

famous_diseases: Harmful to bees on direct contact

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Water-soluble chemical fertilizer - equal NPK mix
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Dissolves in days - leaches into water quickly
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Kills soil bacteria with salt buildup - pollutes nearby water
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - major cause of water pollution

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Water-soluble chemical fertilizer - stronger NPK mix
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Dissolves in days - enters groundwater fast
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Makes soil acidic - kills beneficial microbes over time
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - causes algal blooms in water bodies

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Extremely dangerous weed killer - kills on contact
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Binds to soil particles - stays for years
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Creates toxic oxygen in cells - burns plant tissue instantly
scientific_profile.Global Status: One sip can kill - Banned in Europe, China, Brazil

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Bug killer made from artificial chrysanthemum flower chemicals
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Freezes the nerves of insects - they cannot move
scientific_profile.Global Status: Possibly causes cancer (EPA) - Extremely deadly to fish

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Systemic insect killer - plant absorbs it and becomes poisonous
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays for months to years in soil
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Overstimulates insect nerves until they die - same effect on bees
scientific_profile.Global Status: Main cause of global bee deaths - Banned in Europe (2018)

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Weed killer - stops weed seeds from sprouting
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Stops cells from dividing - weeds cannot grow roots
scientific_profile.Global Status: Possibly causes cancer (EPA) - Restricted in Europe

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Strong nerve poison for insects - very dangerous to children
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Permanently damages nerve connections - brain damage in children
scientific_profile.Global Status: Banned in USA for food crops (2022) - Banned in Europe

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Nitrogen fertilizer - most common in Indian farming
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Converts to ammonia gas in days - much is wasted
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Quick growth burst but kills earthworms and soil bacteria
scientific_profile.Global Status: Subsidized in India (Rs.267/bag) - overused nationwide

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Phosphorus fertilizer - used at sowing time
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays a few weeks in soil
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Contains toxic cadmium metal - builds up in soil over years
scientific_profile.Global Status: Subsidized in India (Rs.1350/bag) - widely used

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Modern insect killer - much safer than older chemicals
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Locks insect muscles - they cannot move or eat
scientific_profile.Global Status: Won Green Chemistry Award - safest synthetic pesticide available

famous_diseases: Very low risk - one of the safest pesticides

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Weed killer - used on maize, sugarcane, and sorghum
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Stays for months to years - most common chemical in drinking water
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Shuts down the plant's energy system - also disrupts animal hormones
scientific_profile.Global Status: Probably causes cancer (WHO-IARC) - Banned in Europe since 2004

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Nitrogen-Sulphur fertilizer - gives quick nitrogen boost
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Makes soil very acidic over time - crops struggle to grow
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - no major bans

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Insect killer - originally copied from sea worm poison
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Blocks nerve signals in insects - they get paralyzed
scientific_profile.Global Status: Restricted in Europe - commonly used in Asia

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Copper-based fungus killer - oldest type of pesticide
scientific_profile.Soil Poison Time: Copper never breaks down - builds up in soil for years
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Copper ions poison fungus cells - but also harms soil life
scientific_profile.Global Status: Even used in organic farming (limited dose) - regulated in Europe

scientific_profile.Chemical Type: Fungus killer - enters through roots and protects from inside
scientific_profile.Attack Method: Stops fungus from making proteins - but diseases develop resistance
scientific_profile.Global Status: Commonly used - but fungi are becoming resistant to it
